matches.name: Microsoft
matches.description: Dominant platform, wide ecosystem moat (Windows / dev tools), secular tailwinds (PC adoption), founder-led with strong capital discipline.

matches.name: Cisco Systems
matches.description: Dominant in networking infrastructure, switching-cost moat (IOS lock-in), secular internet growth, founder-led at vintage date.

matches.name: Adobe Systems
matches.description: Dominant in creative software, ecosystem switching costs, transitioning to SaaS at vintage (secular growth pivot), capable management.

matches.name: Intel Corporation
matches.description: Dominant semis player, wide ecosystem moat (x86), secular PC tailwinds. Demonstrates the regime change risk for hardware moats.

matches.name: Qualcomm
matches.description: IP and standards moat in CDMA, growth phase of mobile, but contested by regulatory action — narrower moat than appearance suggested.

matches.name: EMC Corporation
matches.description: Dominant in enterprise storage, integration moat, but market itself entering disruption (cloud) — cautionary parallel.

pullquote_html: Five of the six closest analogues to NVIDIA-2014 were strong long-term outcomes. The one that wasn't — EMC — telegraphed its risk in a feature the engine sees: market_quality declining from stable to disrupted. The shape of the parallel matters as much as its existence.
